subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Monsters
- **1** village game board (interconnected locations)
- **6** monster figures: Dracula, Frankenstein's Monster, The Bride of Frankenstein, The Wolf Man, The Invisible Man, The Creature from the Black Lagoon
- **6** monster mats (unique defeat conditions)

#### Cards & Items
- **Item cards** — color-coded items used to defeat monsters and complete tasks
- **Monster cards** — drawn each turn, control monster movement and events
- **Perk cards** — special one-time abilities for heroes
- **Hero cards** — 7 different heroes with unique abilities

#### Tokens & Markers
- **Villager tokens** — civilians that must be protected
- **Item tokens** — placed on the board at item locations
- **Terror track marker** — tracks impending doom
- **Monster-specific tokens** — coffin lids for Dracula, body parts for Frankenstein, etc.
- **Frenzy tokens** — for The Wolf Man mechanics

#### Player Components
- **7** hero figures (choose 1 per player)
- **Hero mats** — showing abilities and item capacity

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the village board** — Shows interconnected locations (Church, Graveyard, Manor, Laboratory, Docks, Camp, etc.).
2. **Choose monsters** — Select 1-3 monsters based on desired difficulty:
   - **1 monster** = introductory game
   - **2 monsters** = standard game
   - **3 monsters** = challenging game
3. **Place monster figures** — Put each selected monster on its starting location as shown on its monster mat.
4. **Set up monster tasks** — Place monster-specific tokens according to each monster's mat:
   - **Dracula** — place coffin tokens on marked locations.
   - **Frankenstein & Bride** — scatter body part tokens.
   - **Creature** — place tokens at water locations.
   - **Wolf Man** — no extra tokens needed.
   - **Invisible Man** — place trap tokens.
5. **Terror track** — Set marker to 0. The track goes up to 7 (lose at 7).
6. **Item deck** — Shuffle and place face-down.
7. **Monster deck** — Shuffle and place face-down. Size varies by player count.
8. **Villager tokens** — Place in the supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Chooses
- **1 hero** — Each hero has a unique special ability:
  - **The Mayor** — can give items to distant heroes.
  - **The Inspector** — extra actions when near monsters.
  - **The Archaeologist** — moves items more efficiently.
  - **The Courier** — extra movement.
  - **The Explorer** — can move through blocked paths.
  - **The Scientist** — efficient item use.
  - **The Bold Adventurer** — combat bonuses.
- Place your hero figure on your hero's starting location.
- Take your hero mat.

#### Starting Items
- Draw starting item cards based on player count (fewer players = more starting items).
- Item capacity is limited (typically 4-5 items per hero).

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Cooperative** — All players work together against the monsters.
- **Terror at 0** — No terror yet.
- **Monsters active** — All selected monsters are on the board and will act each turn.
- **Item supply** — Items appear at specific locations on the board.
- **Win condition** — Defeat all selected monsters.
- **Lose condition** — Terror track reaches 7, or a player is defeated with no items to discard.
- **First player** — Chosen randomly.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each player's turn has two phases:

#### Phase 1: Hero Phase (Your Actions)
You get a number of **actions** (typically 3-4 based on player count). Spend actions on:
- **Move** — Move to an adjacent connected location (1 action per space).
- **Pick up item** — Take an item token or card from your location (1 action).
- **Share item** — Give an item to another hero at your location (1 action).
- **Guide villager** — Move a villager one space toward safety (1 action).
- **Advance a task** — Spend items to progress a monster's defeat condition (1 action per item spent).
- **Special ability** — Use your hero's unique ability (varies).

#### Phase 2: Monster Phase
After your actions, draw a **Monster card** and resolve it:
1. **Item placement** — Place new item tokens at locations shown on the card.
2. **Monster event** — The card may trigger a special event.
3. **Monster movement** — Move each active monster toward the nearest hero or villager.
4. **Monster attack** — If a monster is in the same location as a hero, it attacks. The hero must discard items or take damage.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Defeating Monsters (Unique Conditions)
Each monster has a specific defeat condition:

- **Dracula** — Deliver items to each of his 4 coffin locations to destroy them, then confront Dracula with remaining items.
- **Frankenstein's Monster & Bride** — Collect body part tokens and deliver them to the Laboratory. Reunite the monsters at the lab to defeat both.
- **The Creature from the Black Lagoon** — Deliver specific colored items to marked water locations in sequence.
- **The Wolf Man** — Collect items and confront him. He moves erratically based on frenzy tokens.
- **The Invisible Man** — Set traps at locations using items. When the Invisible Man moves onto a trapped location, he becomes visible and can be defeated.
- **The Mummy** — Solve a sliding puzzle by arranging tokens in the correct order using items.

#### Guiding Villagers
- Villagers appear on the board when Monster cards place them.
- If a monster reaches a villager, the villager is **attacked** and the terror track advances.
- Guide villagers to safe locations to prevent terror increases.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Terror Track
- Increases when villagers are attacked or certain Monster card events trigger.
- If it reaches **7**, the team **immediately loses**.
- Managing terror is as important as defeating monsters.

#### Item Colors
- Items come in different colors (red, blue, yellow, green, etc.).
- Monster defeat conditions often require **specific colors** at **specific locations**.
- Planning item logistics is a core challenge.

#### Monster Movement
- Monsters move toward the **nearest hero or villager**.
- Some monsters move faster than others.
- Dracula moves at night (special condition), Wolf Man moves erratically.

#### Player Defeat
- If a monster attacks you and you have **no items to discard**, you are defeated.
- A defeated player is not eliminated — they respawn but the terror track advances.
- With enough items, you can always absorb attacks by discarding items.

#### Perk Cards
- One-time use abilities that can turn the tide.
- Gained through certain conditions or monster-specific events.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
The team **wins** by defeating **all selected monsters** before the terror track reaches 7.

#### Lose Conditions
- **Terror track reaches 7** — village is overrun.
- **Monster deck runs out** — time runs out (optional advanced rule).

#### Difficulty Scaling
- **1 monster** — Easy introduction.
- **2 monsters** — Standard difficulty. Good for learning.
- **3 monsters** — Challenging. Requires coordination.
- Monster combinations also affect difficulty (some monsters are harder than others).

#### No Numeric Score
This is a cooperative win/lose game. There is no point total.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Identify item needs** — Look at each monster's defeat conditions and note which colors and locations are needed.
- **Split responsibilities** — Assign heroes to handle specific monsters or tasks based on proximity and abilities.
- **Protect villagers** — Don't ignore villagers. Terror buildup can end the game before you defeat any monster.
- **Use hero abilities** — Each hero ability is powerful. Position yourself to maximize its effect.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring terror** — Focusing only on monster defeat while villagers get attacked leads to a terror loss.
- **Hoarding items** — Items in your hand don't help the board. Deliver them to task locations efficiently.
- **Poor coordination** — Working on the same monster when you should split efforts wastes actions.
- **Forgetting item capacity** — You can only hold 4-5 items. Don't over-collect.
- **Neglecting movement efficiency** — Plan routes to pick up items AND deliver them in the same trip.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which monsters to choose** — Monster selection determines difficulty and strategy.
- **Which monster to defeat first** — Removing one monster reduces threats. Prioritize the most dangerous.
- **Item routing** — Efficient item pickup and delivery is the core puzzle.
- **When to protect vs. progress** — Sometimes you must pause your monster task to save a villager.
- **Hero selection** — Choose heroes whose abilities complement the chosen monsters.
